events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: confined space work in progress, permit expired mid-shift. Under OSHA 1910.146, entry permit is valid only for the duration of the task. If permit expires, work must stop, space evacuated, permit re-evaluated/reissued before re-entry. This is a sequence violation - work continuing with expired permit is non-compliant and dangerous.

Decision: fail
Risk: high/critical - working in confined space without valid permit is critical.
Component conditions: non-compliant
Work stage: in-progress
Percent complete: estimate maybe 50
Actions: stop work, evacuate space, re-evaluate conditions, reissue permit, verify atmospheric testing, then re-enter.
Findings: permit expired mid-shift, work continued without valid permit.
References: OSHA 1910.146

    "Work continued without a valid entry permit, violating permit-required confined space procedures",
    "Atmospheric monitoring and entry authorization lapse with permit expiration"

  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop all confined space work and evacuate entrants from the space",
    "Cancel the expired permit and perform a new hazard assessment of the space",
    "Conduct fresh atmospheric testing and verify ventilation before any re-entry",
    "Reissue a new entry permit signed by the entry supervisor before work resumes",
    "Verify attendant and rescue provisions are in place under the new permit"
  ],
  "rationale": "Under OSHA 1910.146, a permit-required confined space entry is only authorized for the duration listed on the permit. Once the permit expires, the entry is no longer authorized and all entrants must leave the space. Continuing work with an expired permit is a critical sequence/compliance violation; a new permit, re-testing, and re-authorization must occur before re-entry.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146 - Permit-Required Confined Spaces",
    "OSHA 1910.146(e) - Entry permit",
    "OSHA 1910.146(f) - Entry permit duration and cancellation"
  ]
}
